title: One structure for everything
blurb: The Logic Graph: one structure holding programs, types, the standard library, and the compiler's own logic, all traversed by the same operations.

title: The mutability and construction model
blurb: mut as a recursive type modifier and an undefined-to-defined-to-frozen lifecycle, so immutability is the default and frozen is final.

title: Source and runtime graphs
blurb: A persistent file-backed source graph that is the program's identity, plus an ephemeral arena-allocated runtime graph cleaned up by scope lifetimes, no GC.

title: Syntax is data
blurb: The grammar lives in the graph, so a new operator, constructor, or macro is ordinary library work rather than a change to the language.

title: The identity-recognition engine
blurb: One engine for parsing, reflection, and rewrite matching: a merged, stratified recognizer that returns every identity matching at a node.

title: Incremental re-identification
blurb: Edits touch only the tokens they change; each cached identity is a reader, invalidated exactly when the tokens or rules it consulted change.

title: Interpret by default, JIT on demand
blurb: Logic Graph code is interpreted directly; a frozen region JIT-compiles via Cranelift and stays reflectable through the graph it came from. Hot regions are prom…

title: Compile-time evaluation and metaprogramming
blurb: A comptime marker bakes results into the artifact, with the whole language available at parse time over real Logic Graph rather than a macro sublanguage.

title: The compiler is a library
blurb: The checker, optimizer, and the lowerings to native code are ordinary Logos over the graph, not a sealed black box.

title: One rewriting engine
blurb: A single equality-saturation engine for compiler optimization, computer algebra, and user transforms, with first-class rule sets and cost functions.

title: The Logos IR
blurb: A target-agnostic intermediate layer with explicit basic blocks, control flow, memory operations, and types, itself a stratum of the graph. One backend interfa…

title: User-replaceable allocators
blurb: Arena, bump, pool, system, and custom allocators behind one interface, with arenas carrying the graph's cyclic structures.

title: The borrow checker, one read/write rule
blurb: Lexical lifetimes, ownership, and moves with no GC; place- and reference-granular borrows; the same many-readers-or-one-writer rule that also governs visibilit…

title: Multithreading and task scheduling
blurb: Two concurrency shapes the borrow checker proves race-free. parallel for spreads work over disjoint indices, with threads reading shared graph structure throug…

title: Capability and effect tracking
blurb: Effects as capabilities (pure, total, async, thread-pinned, GPU-subset, allocator-bound) tracked through the call graph, so compile-time code is I/O-free and b…

title: Pay only for what you verify
blurb: Opt-in strata: refinement types with SMT discharge, then pre- and post-conditions, then termination measures. Lower code is never burdened by higher guarantees.

title: Dependent types and a proof kernel
blurb: Propositions as types and proofs as programs, checked by a small trusted kernel and erased before codegen, with a stratified universe hierarchy.

title: The ecumenical proof system
blurb: Many theories in one graph, including contradictory ones, each proof carrying the axioms it rests on so that only consistent results combine.

title: Errors are values
blurb: Fallible functions return tagged unions (T | Error), handled explicitly via match; no exceptions and no implicit propagation in the core.

title: Native interop and the Rust bridge
blurb: Calling existing native code in-process across the C ABI with no linker step, independent of which backend compiled either side.

title: A batteries-included standard library
blurb: Single canonical solutions decided and documented before release: collections, strings, numerics, symbolic math, allocators, concurrency, I/O, and verification.

title: LSP-first editor support
blurb: A Logos-written language server bringing rich highlighting, errors, autocomplete, and refactoring to any LSP editor.

title: Debugger and profiler integration
blurb: Standard-protocol DAP debugging, samplers, and flamegraphs, so users keep the tools they already know.

title: A documentation generator
blurb: Generated from the same graph that holds types, examples, capabilities, and proofs, so docs carry verified examples and proof obligations.

title: A Logos-native structural editor
blurb: Semantic editing directly on Logic Graphs and an IDE customizable in Logos: the long-term goal, not on the critical path.

title: the Lexer
blurb: The lexer is based on a trie just that each node can also be a regex pattern so that nearly all string identifications you want to do could be represented. Sin…

title: A seed that self-hosts
blurb: A minimal Rust seed bootstraps the system and processes Logos until it compiles itself; its primitives ship as opaque native code and are ported to reflectable…
